skill.name: python-development-python-scaffold
skill.description: You are a Python project architecture expert specializing in scaffolding production-ready Python applications. Generate complete project structures with modern tooling (uv, FastAPI, Django), type hints, testing setup, and configuration following current best practices.
skill.summary: Expert Python project scaffolding with modern tooling like uv, FastAPI, Django, type hints, and testing setup.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 35 patterns as potential security issues, but all are false positives. The 'external_commands' are documentation examples showing users bash commands to run. The 'network' and 'env_access' findings reference example templates in .env.example files. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections match description strings, not actual crypto code. This skill only generates text output and code templates with no execution capabilities.

content.user_title: Generate production-ready Python project structures instantly
content.value_statement: Setting up Python projects with proper structure and tooling is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill instantly generates complete, production-ready project scaffolds with modern best practices built in.
actual_capabilities: Generate FastAPI project structures with async support, API versioning, and SQLAlchemy integration
Create Django project layouts with proper settings configuration and app structure
Build Python library packages with pyproject.toml, type stubs, and publication-ready setup
Scaffold CLI tools using Typer with Rich console output and command groups
Configure development tooling including ruff, pytest, mypy, and uv virtual environments
Generate .env.example templates, Makefiles, and comprehensive .gitignore files
limitations: Generates project templates and code structures only, does not execute commands on your system
Focuses on Python projects only, does not support other programming languages
Provides standard project layouts that may require customization for complex enterprise requirements
Example configurations use common defaults that should be reviewed for your specific security needs

use_cases.title: Rapid API Development
use_cases.description: Quickly scaffold new FastAPI microservices with proper structure, database integration, and testing setup for teams shipping multiple services.

use_cases.title: Open Source Library Creation
use_cases.description: Generate publication-ready Python package structures with correct pyproject.toml configuration, type hints, and testing infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Django Web Application Setup
use_cases.description: Create well-organized Django projects with environment configuration, debug tooling, and app separation following Django best practices.

prompt_templates.title: Basic FastAPI Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a FastAPI project structure for a user management API with PostgreSQL database integration and JWT authentication.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to start a new REST API project with standard authentication patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Django Project with Apps
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Django project structure with separate apps for users, products, and orders. Include environment configuration and development tooling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a full-stack e-commerce application requiring multiple Django apps.

prompt_templates.title: Python CLI Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Scaffold a CLI tool with Typer that accepts user input and displays formatted output. Include subcommands for different operations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a command-line utility for automation tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Reusable Python Library
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Python library package with full type hinting, pytest setup, mypy configuration, and pyproject.toml for PyPI publication.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a reusable utility library to share across multiple projects.

output_examples.input: Generate a FastAPI project with user endpoints and PostgreSQL
output_examples.output: Complete directory structure with src/project_name/ containing main.py, config.py, api/v1/endpoints/users.py, models/user.py, schemas/user.py, plus pyproject.toml with FastAPI and SQLAlchemy dependencies, and tests/ with conftest.py fixtures.

output_examples.input: Create a CLI tool structure with Typer
output_examples.output: Project layout with cli.py using Typer decorators, command groups for different operations, Rich console formatting, and pyproject.toml with script entry point configuration.

best_practices: Use uv for dependency management to benefit from fast lock file resolution and modern Python packaging
Structure projects with src/ layout for clean imports and to avoid accidental local module shadowing
Include comprehensive type hints and configure mypy strict mode for catching type errors early
anti_patterns: Do not skip the .env.example file - always document required environment variables for team members
Avoid placing application code in the project root - use src/ layout for proper package structure
Do not commit .env files with actual secrets - only commit templates with placeholder values

faq.question: What Python versions does this skill support?
faq.answer: The generated projects target Python 3.11 and above, which is the current recommended version for new projects. You can modify the requires-python field in pyproject.toml if you need different version support.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands on my system?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates text output showing project structures, file contents, and commands you should run. You manually execute the suggested commands in your terminal.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with existing projects?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for new project scaffolding. For existing projects, consider using it as a reference for restructuring or adding missing components like testing setup or type hints.

faq.question: What database backends are supported?
faq.answer: The FastAPI templates include SQLAlchemy which supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and other databases. The example configurations use PostgreSQL but can be easily adapted to other databases.

faq.question: Does the generated code include authentication?
faq.answer: The templates include structure for authentication (security.py, deps.py for dependencies) but you need to implement your specific authentication logic. JWT and OAuth2 patterns are commonly used with FastAPI.

faq.question: How do I customize the generated project structure?
faq.answer: Treat the generated structure as a starting point. Add or remove directories, modules, and dependencies based on your project needs. The skill follows common conventions that you can modify.
